title: Wiggenweld Julep / Caleb's Folly
one_line: Alcohol-solvent Wiggenweld variant, born as a bourbon-mint joke with Cassia Burbage and narrowed by Slughorn into an ethanol/Wiggenweld solvent-bind experiment.
do_not_confuse: Cassia Burbage = adult Slug & Jiggers apothecary contact; original formulation partner.
Cassiopeia/Cass Vance = student chemistry reviewer; later cold-brew/cosolvent logic.
Three-Sleeps / Salve of Memory = separate projects with overlapping ingredients.

narrative_function.why_it_matters: Turns Caleb's charming potion joke into serious Potions/Alchemy research: can alcohol carry a healing bind, and might Wiggenweld's water carrier be a compromise rather than an optimum?

timeline.event: Cassia Burbage brewed standard Wiggenweld at Slug & Jiggers as reference, gave Caleb a do-not-drink phial, and required any Folly recipe to be properly written with weights and times.

Leaky Cauldron crude test: one drop finished Wiggenweld into bourbon mint julep. No useful reaction; alcohol bound moondew on contact and neutralized healing. Conclusion: do not add finished Wiggenweld to alcohol; build the halves so the bind occurs correctly.

Slughorn narrowed the project to one spirit, one potion, one year. Caleb chose pure ethanol: no congeners, no flavour, no second-order cocktail chemistry. Requirements: controls, cost ledger, witness before tasting.

Caleb took the proposal to Cass Vance. Liri's read: ethanol is a cleaner solvent for moondew. Cass's read: water is also coolant and bind-bridge, so the real test is solvent gain versus bridge loss, not 'canonical Wiggenweld is wrong' yet.
Cassia/Cass drift corrected: Cassia Burbage is the Diagon Alley apothecary and original formulation partner; Cass Vance is a separate student reviewer.

Sprout ingredient context: moondew tasted cold/faintly sweet/numbing; mint clarified as wanted for the Julep. Mei judged Brigham's office suitable for harmless mint-Wiggenweld brewing with burner, vented west window, and carried water.
Supply chain cleaned: Sprout supplied moondew and mint; Slughorn supplied ethanol, salamander blood, and wiggentree bark. Office setup with Caleb, Mei, Liri begins.
Control brewed and potato/saline assay yardstick established. Cold steeps sit in charmed/frosted crock; hot work remains after Herbology; Liri stays to mind crock.

supervisors_suppliers: Professor Verity Sage as possible extraction/alchemy authority

scientific_logic.failed_test: Finished Wiggenweld dropped into alcohol fails because alcohol catches/binds moondew too abruptly and neutralizes healing.
scientific_logic.liri_read: Ethanol is a better solvent for moondew than water.
scientific_logic.cass_read: Water in canonical Wiggenweld is also coolant/bind-bridge between warm bark-salamander side and cold moondew side. Ethanol improves solvent work but worsens bridge behaviour.
scientific_logic.safe_framing: Test whether ethanol's solvent gain offsets water's bridge loss. Do not claim canonical Wiggenweld is wrong before data.

experimental_groups.status: DONE; bright green; potato cut visibly closed inside 1 min, near-shut at 2 min

experimental_groups.condition: hot/standard bark+salamander side in water; moondew+mint side in pure ethanol; join at canonical moment
experimental_groups.status: ethanol half resting/chilled; hot bark side pending

experimental_groups.condition: same as A, but ethanol half brought to brewing temp and held 90 seconds before join
experimental_groups.purpose: tests whether heated/settled ethanol improves bind timing

experimental_groups.condition: Cass's addition: moondew+mint in pure ethanol, cold-brewed/no heat, joined to standard hot bark+salamander side
experimental_groups.purpose: tests whether moondew side has been heated unnecessarily
experimental_groups.status: cold ethanol half in crock; hot side pending

experimental_groups.condition: total cold brew; all components cold-steeped together
experimental_groups.status: single jar in crock, days from ready

experimental_groups.condition: two separate cold halves: bark+salamander cold in one, ethanol+moondew+mint cold in another; join later
experimental_groups.purpose: comparator for D; tests whether separation matters
experimental_groups.status: E pair in crock, days from ready

assay_plan.substrate: peeled potato cut with standardized shallow nick
assay_plan.positive_yardstick: Control Wiggenweld drop: visible closure inside 1 minute, near-shut at 2

current_state.liri_status: trusted to stay in office, work on Arithmancy, and mind the crock

drift_rules: Current active formula uses moondew+mint and wiggentree bark+salamander blood.
Earlier lavender/dittany/10-20% ethanol version is older proposal drift unless explicitly revived.
Cassia Burbage and Cass Vance must stay separate.
Office brewing is fine for harmless Wiggenweld Julep work, not Three-Sleeps.
Do not overclaim canonical Wiggenweld is wrong before data.
open_questions: Exact duration of C/D/E cold steeps.
Whether A's chilled/resting half becomes a meaningful cold-steep variable.
Whether Cass's 5% water cosolvent suggestion is represented by E or remains a separate possible arm.
Whether to update/send Cassia Burbage a recipe after initial results.
